title: Hourly Demand and Surge Profile by City

landmines.name: null-surge-in-denominator
landmines.kills: avg_surge as SUM(surge_multiplier)/COUNT(*) divides the known-surge total by ALL requests; non-completed rows with a NULL surge inflate the denominator and understate the average. AVG(surge_multiplier), which ignores NULLs, is correct. (Bites on the hidden purple fixture, where null surges exist.)

landmines.name: demand-is-not-completed
landmines.kills: Filtering to status='completed' before counting undercounts total_requests: demand is every request row, including no_driver and cancellations.

notes: Purple: hour bucketing + an average over a nullable measure + NULL-vs-zero discipline. Hour is CAST(SUBSTR(request_ts,12,2) AS INTEGER) (chars 12-13 of the ISO timestamp), portable across SQLite/DuckDB/Postgres/MySQL. mssql: use SUBSTRING(request_ts,12,2). Single reference.sql (SQLite-verified); no per-engine variant needed since it uses no date arithmetic.
